name: La Calzadilla Trail
name_original: Castillo de Huerta de la Obispalía — circular desde Poveda de la Obispalía
description: Explore the easy 13.6-mile La Calzadilla Trail by mountain bike, featuring the historic Castle and church of Huerta de la Obispalía.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Altarejos offers a network of routes through rural landscapes. The region features rolling terrain with varied elevation changes, suitable for different skill levels. Paths often traverse open fields, agricultural areas, and sections of woodland, providing a mix of surfaces from gravel tracks to dirt trails. The area's topography includes gentle ascents and descents, making it accessible for both recreational rides and more extended excursions. Best mountain bike trails around Altarejos The most popular mountain bike trail is La Calzadilla Trail, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route features gentle gradients and open views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fuente de Huerta – Poveda de la Obispalía loop from Altarejos, a moderate 25.9 miles (41.7 km) path. This route offers a mix of rural tracks and some climbs, passing through varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Centennial Pine Near Malpensa – Ermita de Malpensa loop from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla, a 25.6 miles (41.3 km) trail leading through agricultural land and scattered pine areas, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Altarejos is defined by rural tracks, open fields, and sections of woodland, with options for various ability levels. The network includes easy routes and more challenging moderate paths. The routes in Altarejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Altarejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Altarejos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Altarejos are predominantly moderate, with 7 out of 8 routes falling into this category. There is also one easy trail, making the area suitable for riders with some experience, as well as those looking for a gentler introduction to mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Altarejos?
A: Yes, beginners can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017976767/la-calzadilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Calzadilla Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This easy-rated route is 13.6 miles (21.9 km) long and features gentle gradients, offering a pleasant ride through open countryside.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Altarejos?
A: The terrain around Altarejos features rolling landscapes with varied elevation changes. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including gravel tracks and dirt trails, often traversing open fields, agricultural areas, and sections of woodland. The routes generally include gentle ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Altarejos?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Huerta – Poveda de la Obispalía loop from Altarejos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.9 miles (41.7 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centennial Pine Near Malpensa – Ermita de Malpensa loop from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla&lt;/a&gt;, covering 25.6 miles (41.3 km).

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter several points of interest. Notable attractions in the wider area include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and church of Huerta de la Obispalía&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6655780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Roque Hermitage and Rock-Cut Tombs&lt;/a&gt;, and the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5769554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra de Záncara&lt;/a&gt;. These offer glimpses into the region's history and culture.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Altarejos?
A: The komoot community rates the mountain bike trails in Altarejos with an average score of 3.0 stars. Riders often appreciate the rural tracks, open fields, and woodland sections that define the area, providing a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for extended excursions?
A: Yes, for longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde de Júcar – Valverde de Júcar loop from Belmontejo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 32.4 miles (52.2 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a substantial ride through the varied landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Altarejos?
A: Given the rural and open nature of the trails, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable during those months. Winter rides are possible, but check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Altarejos?
A: While the area is generally not heavily trafficked, for a potentially quieter experience, consider exploring some of the moderate routes further from the main villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Malpensa – Centennial Pine Near Malpensa loop from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla&lt;/a&gt;, at 21.2 miles (34.1 km), offers a good option for a more secluded ride.

Q: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride in Altarejos during summer?
A: During summer, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and light, breathable clothing. Given the rural nature, a basic repair kit, spare tube, and a fully charged phone are also highly recommended. There are limited facilities directly on trails, so plan accordingly.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Altarejos?
A: There are 8 mountain bike trails documented around Altarejos on komoot, offering a range of options for different skill levels and preferences.
tips: The ruins of the old castle stand out as they are on the top of the small hill and next to them is the Renaissance church from the 16th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike La Calzadilla Trail?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 21.9 kilometers long and generally takes around 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, skill level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is La Calzadilla Trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is rated as easy, making it suitable for beginner mountain bikers. It offers a manageable distance and elevation gain, providing a good experience for those new to the sport.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, La Calzadilla Trail is designed as a circular route, meaning you will finish your ride close to where you started. This makes for convenient planning without the need for shuttle services.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead for La Calzadilla Trail and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near Poveda de la Obispalía. While specific parking details are not widely documented, it's common for trails in such areas to have informal parking near the starting point in or around the village. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty rating for mountain biking, you can expect mostly well-maintained paths, possibly including gravel roads, dirt tracks, and some smoother singletrack sections. The route features a moderate elevation gain of about 258 meters, suggesting rolling hills rather than steep climbs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike La Calzadilla Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail typically depends on the local climate. Spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential wet, muddy conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant point of interest along the route is the Castle and church of Huerta de la Obispalía. This historical highlight offers a chance to pause and appreciate the local heritage and scenery.

faqs.question: Does La Calzadilla Trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 16% of the route follows the path named 'La Calzadilla' itself, and a smaller portion (1%) uses 'Camino del cementerio'.

faqs.question: Is La Calzadilla Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this particular trail are not widely published, many rural trails in the region are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Please be mindful of any livestock you may encounter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access La Calzadilla Trail. It appears to be a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For an easy mountain bike ride of this length, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation is also advisable.

waypoints.name: Castle and church of Huerta de la Obispalía
